# Third-party vendored assets — wpkdraw

Two files are vendored here for wpkdraw's font engine. Both ship under
permissive licenses compatible with this repo. Neither file is modified
from upstream.

## `stb_truetype.h`

- Upstream: <https://github.com/nothings/stb/blob/master/stb_truetype.h>
- Version: v1.26 (header self-reports the version at line 1).
- License: dual-licensed under MIT and public-domain Unlicense — see the
  trailing `ALTERNATIVE A` / `ALTERNATIVE B` license block in the file
  itself. We use the public-domain leg.
- Author: Sean Barrett / RAD Game Tools, 2009-2021.

`stb_truetype` is header-only. Exactly one translation unit
(`build/wpk_stb_impl.c`, generated by `build.sh`) defines
`STB_TRUETYPE_IMPLEMENTATION` before including the header, so the
implementation lands once in `libwpkdraw.a`.

## `Inconsolata-Regular.ttf`

- Upstream: <https://github.com/google/fonts/tree/main/ofl/inconsolata>
- License: SIL Open Font License v1.1
  (<https://openfontlicense.org/open-font-license-official-text/>).
- Authors: Raph Levien with The Inconsolata Project Authors.

Inconsolata is a monospace face — the right default for wpkdraw's terminal
consumer (`wlterm`). The TTF is converted to a C byte array
(`wpk_font_ttf.h`, emitted next to this file) by `build.sh`, which
regenerates it whenever the `.ttf` is newer. The generated header is
git-ignored — the `.ttf` is the source of truth.

We do not modify the font binary, so the SIL OFL reserved-name clause does
not apply. The OFL requires attribution wherever the font is distributed,
which this NOTICE provides.
